NKJV.1: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and talked with me, saying to me, “Come, I will show you the judgment of the great harlot who sits on many waters,
NKJV.2: with whom the kings of the earth committed fornication, and the inhabitants of the earth were made drunk with the wine of her fornication.”
NKJV.3: So he carried me away in the Spirit into the wilderness. And I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast which was full of names of blasphemy, having seven heads and ten horns.
NKJV.4: The woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet, and adorned with gold and precious stones and pearls, having in her hand a golden cup full of abominations and the filthiness of her fornication.
NKJV.5: And on her forehead a name was written: MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH.
NKJV.6: I saw the woman, drunk with the blood of the saints and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus. And when I saw her, I marveled with great amazement.
NKJV.7: But the angel said to me, “Why did you marvel? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and of the beast that carries her, which has the seven heads and the ten horns.
NKJV.8: The beast that you saw was, and is not, and will ascend out of the bottomless pit and go to perdition. And those who dwell on the earth will marvel, whose names are not written in the Book of Life from the foundation of the world, when they see the beast that was, and is not, and yet is.
NKJV.9: “Here is the mind which has wisdom: The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman sits.
NKJV.10: There are also seven kings. Five have fallen, one is, and the other has not yet come. And when he comes, he must continue a short time.
NKJV.11: The beast that was, and is not, is himself also the eighth, and is of the seven, and is going to perdition.
NKJV.12: “The ten horns which you saw are ten kings who have received no kingdom as yet, but they receive authority for one hour as kings with the beast.
NKJV.13: These are of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
NKJV.14: These will make war with the Lamb, and the Lamb will overcome them, for He is Lord of lords and King of kings; and those who are with Him are called, chosen, and faithful.”
NKJV.15: Then he said to me, “The waters which you saw, where the harlot sits, are peoples, multitudes, nations, and tongues.
NKJV.16: And the ten horns which you saw on the beast, these will hate the harlot, make her desolate and naked, eat her flesh and burn her with fire.
NKJV.17: For God has put it into their hearts to fulfill His purpose, to be of one mind, and to give their kingdom to the beast, until the words of God are fulfilled.
NKJV.18: And the woman whom you saw is that great city which reigns over the kings of the earth.”

ESV.1: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and said to me, “Come, I will show you the judgment of the great prostitute who is seated on many waters,
ESV.2: with whom the kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality, and with the wine of whose sexual immorality the dwellers on earth have become drunk.”
ESV.3: And he carried me away in the Spirit into a wilderness, and I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast that was full of blasphemous names, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
ESV.4: The woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet, and adorned with gold and jewels and pearls, holding in her hand a golden cup full of abominations and the impurities of her sexual immorality.
ESV.5: And on her forehead was written a name of mystery: “Babylon the great, mother of prostitutes and of earth's abominations.”
ESV.6: And I saw the woman, drunk with the blood of the saints, the blood of the martyrs of Jesus.  When I saw her, I marveled greatly.
ESV.7: But the angel said to me, “Why do you marvel? I will tell you the mystery of the woman, and of the beast with seven heads and ten horns that carries her.
ESV.8: The beast that you saw was, and is not, and is about to rise from the bottomless pit  and go to destruction. And the dwellers on earth whose names have not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world will marvel to see the beast, because it was and is not and is to come.
ESV.9: This calls for a mind with wisdom: the seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated;
ESV.10: they are also seven kings, five of whom have fallen, one is, the other has not yet come, and when he does come he must remain only a little while.
ESV.11: As for the beast that was and is not, it is an eighth but it belongs to the seven, and it goes to destruction.
ESV.12: And the ten horns that you saw are ten kings who have not yet received royal power, but they are to receive authority as kings for one hour, together with the beast.
ESV.13: These are of one mind, and they hand over their power and authority to the beast.
ESV.14: They will make war on the Lamb, and the Lamb will conquer them, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings, and those with him are called and chosen and faithful.”
ESV.15: And the angel  said to me, “The waters that you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples and multitudes and nations and languages.
ESV.16: And the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute. They will make her desolate and naked, and devour her flesh and burn her up with fire,
ESV.17: for God has put it into their hearts to carry out his purpose by being of one mind and handing over their royal power to the beast, until the words of God are fulfilled.
ESV.18: And the woman that you saw is the great city that has dominion over the kings of the earth.”

NASB.1: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and spoke with me, saying, 'Come here, I will show you the judgment of the great harlot who sits on many waters,
NASB.2: with whom the kings of the earth committed [acts of] immorality, and those who dwell on the earth were made drunk with the wine of her immorality.'
NASB.3: And he carried me away in the Spirit into a wilderness; and I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast, full of blasphemous names, having seven heads and ten horns.
NASB.4: The woman was clothed in purple and scarlet, and adorned with gold and precious stones and pearls, having in her hand a gold cup full of abominations and of the unclean things of her immorality,
NASB.5: and on her forehead a name [was] written, a mystery, 'BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH.'
NASB.6: And I saw the woman drunk with the blood of the saints, and with the blood of the witnesses of Jesus. When I saw her, I wondered greatly.
NASB.7: And the angel said to me, 'Why do you wonder? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and of the beast that carries her, which has the seven heads and the ten horns.
NASB.8: 'The beast that you saw was, and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. And those who dwell on the earth, whose name has not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world, will wonder when they see the beast, that he was and is not and will come.
NASB.9: 'Here is the mind which has wisdom. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman sits,
NASB.10: and they are seven kings; five have fallen, one is, the other has not yet come; and when he comes, he must remain a little while.
NASB.11: 'The beast which was and is not, is himself also an eighth and is [one] of the seven, and he goes to destruction.
NASB.12: 'The ten horns which you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom, but they receive authority as kings with the beast for one hour.
NASB.13: 'These have one purpose, and they give their power and authority to the beast.
NASB.14: 'These will wage war against the Lamb, and the Lamb will overcome them, because He is Lord of lords and King of kings, and those who are with Him [are the] called and chosen and faithful.'
NASB.15: And he said to me, 'The waters which you saw where the harlot sits, are peoples and multitudes and nations and tongues.
NASB.16: 'And the ten horns which you saw, and the beast, these will hate the harlot and will make her desolate and naked, and will eat her flesh and will burn her up with fire.
NASB.17: 'For God has put it in their hearts to execute His purpose by having a common purpose, and by giving their kingdom to the beast, until the words of God will be fulfilled.
NASB.18: 'The woman whom you saw is the great city, which reigns over the kings of the earth.'

NLT.1: One of the seven angels who had poured out the seven bowls came over and spoke to me. “Come with me,” he said, “and I will show you the judgment that is going to come on the great prostitute, who rules over many waters.
NLT.2: The kings of the world have committed adultery with her, and the people who belong to this world have been made drunk by the wine of her immorality.”
NLT.3: So the angel took me in the Spirit into the wilderness. There I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast that had seven heads and ten horns, and blasphemies against God were written all over it.
NLT.4: The woman wore purple and scarlet clothing and beautiful jewelry made of gold and precious gems and pearls. In her hand she held a gold goblet full of obscenities and the impurities of her immorality.
NLT.5: A mysterious name was written on her forehead: “Babylon the Great, Mother of All Prostitutes and Obscenities in the World.”
NLT.6: I could see that she was drunk — drunk with the blood of God’s holy people who were witnesses for Jesus. I stared at her in complete amazement.
NLT.7: “Why are you so amazed?” the angel asked. “I will tell you the mystery of this woman and of the beast with seven heads and ten horns on which she sits.
NLT.8: The beast you saw was once alive but isn’t now. And yet he will soon come up out of the bottomless pit and go to eternal destruction. And the people who belong to this world, whose names were not written in the Book of Life before the world was made, will be amazed at the reappearance of this beast who had died.
NLT.9: “This calls for a mind with understanding: The seven heads of the beast represent the seven hills where the woman rules. They also represent seven kings.
NLT.10: Five kings have already fallen, the sixth now reigns, and the seventh is yet to come, but his reign will be brief.
NLT.11: “The scarlet beast that was, but is no longer, is the eighth king. He is like the other seven, and he, too, is headed for destruction.
NLT.12: The ten horns of the beast are ten kings who have not yet risen to power. They will be appointed to their kingdoms for one brief moment to reign with the beast.
NLT.13: They will all agree to give him their power and authority.
NLT.14: Together they will go to war against the Lamb, but the Lamb will defeat them because he is Lord of all lords and King of all kings. And his called and chosen and faithful ones will be with him.”
NLT.15: Then the angel said to me, “The waters where the prostitute is ruling represent masses of people of every nation and language.
NLT.16: The scarlet beast and his ten horns all hate the prostitute. They will strip her naked, eat her flesh, and burn her remains with fire.
NLT.17: For God has put a plan into their minds, a plan that will carry out his purposes. They will agree to give their authority to the scarlet beast, and so the words of God will be fulfilled.
NLT.18: And this woman you saw in your vision represents the great city that rules over the kings of the world.”

WEB.1: One of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and spoke with me, saying, “Come here. I will show you the judgment of the great prostitute who sits on many waters,
WEB.2: with whom the kings of the earth committed sexual immorality, and those who dwell in the earth were made drunken with the wine of her sexual immorality.”
WEB.3: He carried me away in the Spirit into a wilderness. I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet-colored animal, full of blasphemous names, having seven heads and ten horns.
WEB.4: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet, and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls, having in her hand a golden cup full of abominations and the impurities of the sexual immorality of the earth.
WEB.5: And on her forehead a name was written, “MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF THE PROSTITUTES AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH.”
WEB.6: I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus. When I saw her, I wondered with great amazement.
WEB.7: The angel said to me, “Why do you wonder? I will tell you the mystery of the woman, and of the beast that carries her, which has the seven heads and the ten horns.
WEB.8: The beast that you saw was, and is not; and is about to come up out of the abyss and to go into destruction. Those who dwell on the earth and whose names have not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world will marvel when they see that the beast was, and is not, and shall be present.
WEB.9: Here is the mind that has wisdom. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman sits.
WEB.10: They are seven kings. Five have fallen, the one is, the other has not yet come. When he comes, he must continue a little while.
WEB.11: The beast that was, and is not, is himself also an eighth, and is of the seven; and he goes to destruction.
WEB.12: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings who have received no kingdom as yet, but they receive authority as kings, with the beast, for one hour.
WEB.13: These have one mind, and they give their power and authority to the beast.
WEB.14: These will war against the Lamb, and the Lamb will overcome them, for he is Lord of lords, and King of kings, and those who are with him are called chosen and faithful.”
WEB.15: He said to me, “The waters which you saw, where the prostitute sits, are peoples, multitudes, nations, and languages.
WEB.16: The ten horns which you saw, and the beast, these will hate the prostitute, and will make her desolate, and will make her naked, and will eat her flesh, and will burn her utterly with fire.
WEB.17: For God has put in their hearts to do what he has in mind, and to be of one mind, and to give their kingdom to the beast, until the words of God should be accomplished.
WEB.18: The woman whom you saw is the great city, which reigns over the kings of the earth.”

CSB17.1: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls <sup>ⓓ</sup> came and spoke with me: “Come, I will show you the judgment of the notorious prostitute <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup> who is seated on many <sup>[2]</sup> waters. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: The kings of the earth <sup>ⓖ</sup> committed sexual immorality with her, and those who live on the earth became drunk on the wine of her sexual immorality.” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.3: Then he carried me away in the Spirit <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup> to a wilderness. I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast that was covered <sup>[4]</sup> with blasphemous names and had seven heads and ten horns. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.4: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet, adorned with gold, jewels, and pearls. <sup>ⓚ</sup> She had a golden cup in her hand filled with everything detestable <sup>ⓛ</sup> and with the impurities of her <sup>[5]</sup> prostitution.
CSB17.5: On her forehead was written a name, a mystery: <sup>ⓜ</sup> BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF PROSTITUTES AND OF THE DETESTABLE THINGS OF THE EARTH.
CSB17.6: Then I saw that the woman was drunk with the blood of the saints and with the blood of the witnesses to Jesus. <sup>ⓝ</sup> When I saw her, I was greatly astonished.
CSB17.7: Then the angel said to me, “Why are you astonished? I will explain to you the mystery of the woman and of the beast, with the seven heads and the ten horns, that carries her.
CSB17.8: The beast that you saw was, and is not, and is about to come up from the abyss <sup>ⓐ</sup> and go to destruction. <sup>ⓑ</sup> Those who live on the earth whose names have not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world <sup>ⓒ</sup> will be astonished when they see the beast that was, and is not, and is to come. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.9: This calls for a mind that has wisdom: <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup> “The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated. They are also seven kings:
CSB17.10: Five have fallen, one is, the other has not yet come, and when he comes, he must remain for only a little while.
CSB17.11: The beast that was and is not, is itself an eighth king, but it belongs to the seven and is going to destruction.
CSB17.12: The ten horns you saw are ten kings <sup>ⓕ</sup> who have not yet received a kingdom, but they will receive authority as kings with the beast for one hour. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.13: These have one purpose, and they give their power and authority to the beast.
CSB17.14: These will make war against the Lamb, but the Lamb will conquer them because he is Lord of lords and King of kings. <sup>ⓗ</sup> Those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.” <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.15: He also said to me, “The waters you saw, <sup>ⓙ</sup> where the prostitute was seated, are peoples, multitudes, nations, and languages. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.16: The ten horns you saw, and the beast, will hate the prostitute. <sup>ⓛ</sup> They will make her desolate and naked, devour her flesh, and burn her up with fire. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.17: For God has put it into their hearts to carry out his plan by having one purpose <sup>ⓝ</sup> and to give their kingdom <sup>[7]</sup> to the beast until the words of God are fulfilled. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.18: And the woman you saw is the great city that has royal power over the kings of the earth.”

AMP.1: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and spoke with me, saying, "Come here, I will show you the judgment and doom of the great prostitute who is seated on many waters [influencing nations],
AMP.2: she with whom the kings of the earth have committed acts of immorality, and the inhabitants of the earth have become intoxicated with the wine of her immorality."
AMP.3: And the angel carried me away in the Spirit into a wilderness; and I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast that was entirely covered with blasphemous names, having seven heads and ten horns.
AMP.4: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet, and adorned with gold, precious stones and pearls, [and she was] holding in her hand a gold cup full of the abominations and the filth of her [sexual] immorality.
AMP.5: And on her forehead a name was written, a mystery: "BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF PROSTITUTES (false religions, heresies) AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH."
AMP.6: I saw that the woman was drunk with the blood of the saints (God's people), and with the blood of the witnesses of Jesus [who were martyred]. When I saw her, I wondered in amazement.
AMP.7: But the angel said to me, "Why do you wonder? I will explain to you the mystery of the woman and of the beast that carries her, which has the seven heads and ten horns.
AMP.8: "The beast that you saw was [once], but [now] is not, and he is about to come up out of the abyss (the bottomless pit, the dwelling place of demons) and go to destruction (perdition). And the inhabitants of the earth, whose names have not been written in the Book of Life from the foundation of the world, will be astonished when they see the beast, because he was and is not and is yet to come [to earth].
AMP.9: Here is the mind which has wisdom [and this is what it knows about the vision]. The seven heads are seven hills on which the woman sits;
AMP.10: and they are seven kings: five of whom have fallen, one exists and is reigning; the other [the seventh] has not yet come, and when he does come, he must remain a little while.
AMP.11: And the beast that [once] was but is not, is himself also an eighth king and is one of the seven, and he goes to destruction (perdition).
AMP.12: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom, but [together] they receive authority as kings for a single hour [for a common purpose] along with the beast.
AMP.13: These [kings] have one purpose [one mind, one common goal], and they give their power and authority to the beast.
AMP.14: They will wage war against the Lamb (Christ), and the Lamb will triumph and conquer them, because He is Lord of lords and King of kings, and those who are with Him and on His side are the called and chosen (elect) and faithful."
AMP.15: Then the angel said to me, "The waters which you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples and multitudes and nations and languages.
AMP.16: And the ten horns which you saw, and the beast, these will hate the prostitute and will make her desolate and naked [stripped of her power and influence], and will eat her flesh and completely consume her with fire.
AMP.17: For God has put it in their hearts to carry out His purpose by agreeing together to surrender their kingdom to the beast, until the [prophetic] words of God will be fulfilled.
AMP.18: The woman whom you saw is the great city, which reigns over and dominates and controls the kings and the political leaders of the earth."

MSG.1: One of the Seven Angels who carried the seven bowls came and invited me, "Come, I'll show you the judgment of the great Whore who sits enthroned over many waters,
MSG.2: the Whore with whom the kings of the earth have gone whoring, show you the judgment on earth dwellers drunk on her whorish lust."
MSG.3: In the Spirit he carried me out in the desert. I saw a woman mounted on a Scarlet Beast. Stuffed with blasphemies, the Beast had seven heads and ten horns.
MSG.4: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet, festooned with gold and gems and pearls. She held a gold chalice in her hand, brimming with defiling obscenities, her foul fornications.
MSG.5: A riddle-name was branded on her forehead: great babylon, mother of whores and abominations of the earth.
MSG.6: I could see that the woman was drunk, drunk on the blood of God's holy people, drunk on the blood of the martyrs of Jesus. Astonished, I rubbed my eyes. I shook my head in wonder.
MSG.7: The Angel said, "Does this surprise you? Let me tell you the riddle of the woman and the Beast she rides, the Beast with seven heads and ten horns.
MSG.8: The Beast you saw once was, is no longer, and is about to ascend from the Abyss and head straight for Hell. Earth dwellers whose names weren't written in the Book of Life from the foundation of the world will be dazzled when they see the Beast that once was, is no longer, and is to come.
MSG.9: "But don't drop your guard. Use your head. The seven heads are seven hills; they are where the woman sits.
MSG.10: They are also seven kings: five dead, one living, the other not yet here—and when he does come his time will be brief.
MSG.11: The Beast that once was and is no longer is both an eighth and one of the seven—and headed for Hell.
MSG.12: "The ten horns you saw are ten kings, but they're not yet in power. They will come to power with the Scarlet Beast, but won't last long—a very brief reign.
MSG.13: These kings will agree to turn over their power and authority to the Beast.
MSG.14: They will go to war against the Lamb but the Lamb will defeat them, proof that he is Lord over all lords, King over all kings, and those with him will be the called, chosen, and faithful."
MSG.15: The Angel continued, "The waters you saw on which the Whore was enthroned are peoples and crowds, nations and languages.
MSG.16: And the ten horns you saw, together with the Beast, will turn on the Whore—they'll hate her, violate her, strip her naked, rip her apart with their teeth, then set fire to her.
MSG.17: It was God who put the idea in their heads to turn over their rule to the Beast until the words of God are completed.
MSG.18: The woman you saw is the great city, tyrannizing the kings of the earth."

NIV.1:
The Woman on the Beast
One of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and said to me, “Come, I will show you the punishment of the great prostitute, who sits on many waters.
NIV.2: With her the kings of the earth committed adultery and the inhabitants of the earth were intoxicated with the wine of her adulteries.”
NIV.3: Then the angel carried me away in the Spirit into a desert. There I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names and had seven heads and ten horns.
NIV.4: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet, and was glittering with gold, precious stones and pearls. She held a golden cup in her hand, filled with abominable things and the filth of her adulteries.
NIV.5:
This title was written on her forehead:
mystery
babylon the great
the mother of prostitutes
and of the abominations of the earth.
NIV.6:
I saw that the woman was drunk with the blood of the saints, the blood of those who bore testimony to Jesus.
When I saw her, I was greatly astonished.
NIV.7: Then the angel said to me: “Why are you astonished? I will explain to you the mystery of the woman and of the beast she rides, which has the seven heads and ten horns.
NIV.8: The beast, which you saw, once was, now is not, and will come up out of the Abyss and go to his destruction. The inhabitants of the earth whose names have not been written in the book of life from the creation of the world will be astonished when they see the beast, because he once was, now is not, and yet will come.
NIV.9: “This calls for a mind with wisdom. The seven heads are seven hills on which the woman sits.
NIV.10: They are also seven kings. Five have fallen, one is, the other has not yet come; but when he does come, he must remain for a little while.
NIV.11: The beast who once was, and now is not, is an eighth king. He belongs to the seven and is going to his destruction.
NIV.12: “The ten horns you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom, but who for one hour will receive authority as kings along with the beast.
NIV.13: They have one purpose and will give their power and authority to the beast.
NIV.14: They will make war against the Lamb, but the Lamb will overcome them because he is Lord of lords and King of kings—and with him will be his called, chosen and faithful followers.”
NIV.15: Then the angel said to me, “The waters you saw, where the prostitute sits, are peoples, multitudes, nations and languages.
NIV.16: The beast and the ten horns you saw will hate the prostitute. They will bring her to ruin and leave her naked; they will eat her flesh and burn her with fire.
NIV.17: For God has put it into their hearts to accomplish his purpose by agreeing to give the beast their power to rule, until God’s words are fulfilled.
NIV.18: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.”

NRSVCE.1: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and said to me, “Come, I will show you the judgment of the great whore who is seated on many waters,
NRSVCE.2: with whom the kings of the earth have committed fornication, and with the wine of whose fornication the inhabitants of the earth have become drunk.”
NRSVCE.3: So he carried me away in the spirit into a wilderness, and I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast that was full of blasphemous names, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
NRSVCE.4: The woman was clothed in purple and scarlet, and adorned with gold and jewels and pearls, holding in her hand a golden cup full of abominations and the impurities of her fornication;
NRSVCE.5: and on her forehead was written a name, a mystery: “Babylon the great, mother of whores and of earth’s abominations.”
NRSVCE.6: And I saw that the woman was drunk with the blood of the saints and the blood of the witnesses to Jesus. When I saw her, I was greatly amazed.
NRSVCE.7: But the angel said to me, “Why are you so amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman, and of the beast with seven heads and ten horns that carries her.
NRSVCE.8: The beast that you saw was, and is not, and is about to ascend from the bottomless pit and go to destruction. And the inhabitants of the earth, whose names have not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and is to come.
NRSVCE.9: “This calls for a mind that has wisdom: the seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated; also, they are seven kings,
NRSVCE.10: of whom five have fallen, one is living, and the other has not yet come; and when he comes, he must remain only a little while.
NRSVCE.11: As for the beast that was and is not, it is an eighth but it belongs to the seven, and it goes to destruction.
NRSVCE.12: And the ten horns that you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom, but they are to receive authority as kings for one hour, together with the beast.
NRSVCE.13: These are united in yielding their power and authority to the beast;
NRSVCE.14: they will make war on the Lamb, and the Lamb will conquer them, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings, and those with him are called and chosen and faithful.”
NRSVCE.15: And he said to me, “The waters that you saw, where the whore is seated, are peoples and multitudes and nations and languages.
NRSVCE.16: And the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the whore; they will make her desolate and naked; they will devour her flesh and burn her up with fire.
NRSVCE.17: For God has put it into their hearts to carry out his purpose by agreeing to give their kingdom to the beast, until the words of God will be fulfilled.
NRSVCE.18: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.”

NET.1: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and spoke to me. “Come,” he said, “I will show you the condemnation and punishment of the great prostitute who sits on many waters,
NET.2: with whom the kings of the earth committed sexual immorality and the earth’s inhabitants got drunk with the wine of her immorality.”
NET.3: So he carried me away in the Spirit to a wilderness, and there I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast that was full of blasphemous names and had seven heads and ten horns.
NET.4: Now the woman was dressed in purple and scarlet clothing, and adorned with gold, precious stones, and pearls. She held in her hand a golden cup filled with detestable things and unclean things from her sexual immorality.
NET.5: On her forehead was written a name, a mystery: “Babylon the Great, the Mother of prostitutes and of the detestable things of the earth.”
NET.6: I saw that the woman was drunk with the blood of the saints and the blood of those who testified to Jesus. I was greatly astounded when I saw her.
NET.7: But the angel said to me, “Why are you astounded? I will interpret for you the mystery of the woman and of the beast with the seven heads and ten horns that carries her.
NET.8: The beast you saw was, and is not, but is about to come up from the abyss and then go to destruction. The inhabitants of the earth — all those whose names have not been written in the book of life since the foundation of the world — will be astounded when they see that the beast was, and is not, but is to come.
NET.9: (This requires a mind that has wisdom.) The seven heads are seven mountains the woman sits on. They are also seven kings:
NET.10: five have fallen; one is, and the other has not yet come, but whenever he does come, he must remain for only a brief time.
NET.11: The beast that was, and is not, is himself an eighth king and yet is one of the seven, and is going to destruction.
NET.12: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom, but will receive ruling authority as kings with the beast for one hour.
NET.13: These kings have a single intent, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
NET.14: They will make war with the Lamb, but the Lamb will conquer them, because he is Lord of lords and King of kings, and those accompanying the Lamb are the called, chosen, and faithful.”
NET.15: Then the angel said to me, “The waters you saw (where the prostitute is seated) are peoples, multitudes, nations, and languages.
NET.16: The ten horns that you saw, and the beast — these will hate the prostitute and make her desolate and naked. They will consume her flesh and burn her up with fire.
NET.17: For God has put into their minds to carry out his purpose by making a decision to give their royal power to the beast until the words of God are fulfilled.
NET.18: As for the woman you saw, she is the great city that has sovereignty over the kings of the earth.”
